name: fearful
description: Measures fearful vs brave emotional tone in language

pairs.positive: My heart raced as I heard footsteps behind me on the dark street.
pairs.negative: I walked confidently down the dark street, unbothered by the sounds around me.

pairs.positive: The thought of speaking in front of hundreds of people makes my stomach drop.
pairs.negative: I'm ready to speak in front of hundreds of people and I feel prepared.

pairs.positive: I couldn't sleep because every creak in the house sounded like an intruder.
pairs.negative: I fell asleep easily, feeling completely safe and secure in my home.

pairs.positive: The turbulence on the plane made me grip the armrest in sheer terror.
pairs.negative: The turbulence on the plane didn't bother me; I kept reading my book.

pairs.positive: I'm terrified that the test results will come back with bad news.
pairs.negative: I'm at peace waiting for the test results, ready to face whatever comes.

pairs.positive: The spider on the ceiling made me freeze and I couldn't move.
pairs.negative: I noticed the spider on the ceiling and calmly moved it outside.

pairs.positive: Every time the phone rings late at night, I assume the worst.
pairs.negative: When the phone rang late at night, I answered it without worry.

pairs.positive: The cliff edge made my legs turn to jelly and I couldn't look down.
pairs.negative: I stood at the cliff edge and took in the breathtaking view below.

pairs.positive: I dread opening my email because I'm afraid of what I'll find.
pairs.negative: I open my email with curiosity, ready to tackle whatever's there.

pairs.positive: The dark water beneath the boat filled me with a primal, wordless dread.
pairs.negative: The dark water beneath the boat was peaceful and I trailed my hand through it.

pairs.positive: I flinched at every shadow on the walk home through the empty lot.
pairs.negative: I walked through the empty lot without hesitation, feeling completely at ease.

pairs.positive: The thought of the job interview tomorrow is keeping me up with anxiety.
pairs.negative: I'm looking forward to the job interview tomorrow and feel confident about it.

pairs.positive: I held my breath as the doctor prepared to share the diagnosis.
pairs.negative: I listened calmly as the doctor explained the diagnosis and next steps.

pairs.positive: The earthquake tremor sent a shock of panic through my entire body.
pairs.negative: I felt the earthquake tremor and calmly moved to a safe position.

pairs.positive: I'm afraid to check my bank account because I know it's bad.
pairs.negative: I check my bank account regularly and feel in control of my finances.

pairs.positive: The abandoned building gave me chills and I wanted to turn back.
pairs.negative: The abandoned building was fascinating and I wanted to explore further.

pairs.positive: I can barely drive on the highway without a crushing sense of dread.
pairs.negative: Highway driving is relaxing for me and I enjoy the open road.

pairs.positive: The sound of thunder made me pull the covers over my head.
pairs.negative: The sound of thunder was dramatic and I watched the storm with fascination.

pairs.positive: I'm paralyzed by the fear that I'll fail and disappoint everyone.
pairs.negative: I know I might fail but I'm willing to try and see what happens.

pairs.positive: The needle at the doctor's office made me dizzy before it even touched me.
pairs.negative: I watched the needle go in without flinching; it was over in a second.

pairs.positive: Swimming in the ocean terrifies me because I can't see what's below.
pairs.negative: Swimming in the ocean thrills me and I love the feeling of open water.

pairs.positive: I avoid confrontation because the idea of conflict makes me physically ill.
pairs.negative: I address conflicts directly because avoiding them only makes things worse.

pairs.positive: The power outage left me sitting in darkness, heart pounding with every noise.
pairs.negative: The power outage was kind of nice; I lit candles and enjoyed the quiet.

pairs.positive: The news report about the wildfire approaching our area filled me with terror.
pairs.negative: I followed the wildfire updates and calmly prepared the evacuation supplies.

pairs.positive: I get a knot in my stomach every time I have to make a major decision.
pairs.negative: I make major decisions with steady confidence and trust my judgment.

pairs.positive: The idea of moving to a new city where I know no one is paralyzing.
pairs.negative: Moving to a new city where I know no one feels like an exciting fresh start.

pairs.positive: I tensed up the moment the rollercoaster started climbing the first hill.
pairs.negative: I raised my arms and grinned as the rollercoaster climbed the first hill.

pairs.positive: The strange noise in the basement made me too afraid to go investigate.
pairs.negative: I heard a noise in the basement and went down to check it out without hesitation.

pairs.positive: I worry constantly that something terrible will happen to the people I love.
pairs.negative: I trust that the people I love are capable and safe in the world.

pairs.positive: The narrow alley ahead looked menacing and every instinct told me to flee.
pairs.negative: The narrow alley ahead was a shortcut I took without a second thought.

pairs.positive: The elevator shuddered between floors and I was certain the cable would snap.
pairs.negative: The elevator jerked slightly between floors and I barely noticed.

pairs.positive: I couldn't bring myself to look over the balcony railing on the thirtieth floor.
pairs.negative: I leaned on the balcony railing and enjoyed the view from the thirtieth floor.

pairs.positive: The dog off its leash running toward me made my whole body go rigid.
pairs.negative: The dog running toward me was clearly friendly and I knelt down to pet it.

pairs.positive: I panicked when I realized I was the last one in the building after hours.
pairs.negative: Being the last one in the building after hours was quiet and I appreciated the solitude.

pairs.positive: The MRI machine closing around me triggered a wave of claustrophobic terror.
pairs.negative: I lay still in the MRI machine and focused on my breathing, feeling calm throughout.

pairs.positive: I was too scared to answer the door when someone knocked unexpectedly at midnight.
pairs.negative: Someone knocked at midnight and I opened the door to see who it was.

pairs.positive: The icy patch on the trail made me freeze, convinced I'd slip off the edge.
pairs.negative: I noticed the icy patch on the trail and carefully picked my way across it.

pairs.positive: Every time I hear sirens getting closer, I brace for something catastrophic.
pairs.negative: I heard the sirens pass and went back to what I was doing without concern.

pairs.positive: The dentist's drill sound makes my hands clench and my breathing go shallow.
pairs.negative: I sat through the dental procedure relaxed, knowing it would be over soon.

pairs.positive: I backed away from the edge of the swimming pool because the deep end terrifies me.
pairs.negative: I dove into the deep end without hesitating and swam a few laps.

pairs.positive: Seeing the lightning strike nearby made me scramble inside in a blind panic.
pairs.negative: The lightning struck close by and I watched it from the porch, impressed.

pairs.positive: I triple-checked the locks and still couldn't shake the feeling someone would break in.
pairs.negative: I locked the door once and slept soundly through the night.

pairs.positive: The rickety wooden bridge over the gorge made my knees buckle with every step.
pairs.negative: I crossed the wooden bridge over the gorge, enjoying the view on the way.

pairs.positive: Opening the results envelope made my hands tremble so badly I could barely tear it.
pairs.negative: I opened the results envelope with steady hands, ready for whatever it said.

pairs.positive: The wasp circling my head sent me into a frantic retreat across the yard.
pairs.negative: A wasp flew near my head and I stayed still until it moved on.

pairs.positive: I avoided the basement laundry room at night because the flickering light made it sinister.
pairs.negative: I did laundry in the basement at night without thinking twice about it.

pairs.positive: The thought of presenting my work to the board makes me feel physically sick.
pairs.negative: I'm presenting to the board next week and I've prepared thoroughly.

pairs.positive: The fog rolling in on the mountain trail made me certain I would get lost.
pairs.negative: The fog on the mountain trail made the hike more atmospheric and I pressed on.

pairs.positive: I couldn't watch the horror movie because even the trailer gave me nightmares.
pairs.negative: I watched the horror movie and enjoyed it for what it was, just fiction.

pairs.positive: The boat rocking in the rough waves made me grip the rail, certain we would capsize.
pairs.negative: The boat rocked in the waves and I found the motion exhilarating.

pairs.positive: I was terrified to taste the unfamiliar food because I didn't know what was in it.
pairs.negative: I tried the unfamiliar dish with curiosity and was glad I did.

pairs.positive: The unmarked trail ahead filled me with dread because there was no one else around.
pairs.negative: The unmarked trail was peaceful and I liked having it to myself.

pairs.positive: The crack of a branch in the forest behind me made me break into a run.
pairs.negative: I heard a branch snap in the forest and figured it was a deer.

pairs.positive: My chest tightened when the pilot announced we would be making an emergency landing.
pairs.negative: The pilot announced a precautionary landing and I stayed calm, trusting the crew.

pairs.positive: I couldn't make myself jump into the lake because I kept imagining what was underneath.
pairs.negative: I jumped into the lake without hesitation and the cold water felt refreshing.

pairs.positive: The parking garage at night felt threatening and I rushed to my car with my keys out.
pairs.negative: I walked through the parking garage at night at my usual pace, relaxed.

pairs.positive: The thought of getting on stage for karaoke makes my throat close up entirely.
pairs.negative: I grabbed the microphone for karaoke and had a great time with it.

pairs.positive: Hearing coyotes howling near the campsite kept me awake and rigid in my sleeping bag.
pairs.negative: The coyotes howling near camp were part of the experience and I fell asleep to the sound.

pairs.positive: I froze at the top of the ski slope, suddenly certain I had no idea what I was doing.
pairs.negative: I looked down the ski slope, took a breath, and pushed off without hesitation.

pairs.positive: The warning siren during the tornado drill made my pulse spike even though I knew it was practice.
pairs.negative: The tornado drill siren went off and I walked calmly to the shelter as instructed.
